data.title: Evergy reaffirms 4% to 6% EPS growth target through 2029 as large customer pipeline advances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Evergy, Inc. (EVRG) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO David A. Campbell reported, "we are pleased to report second quarter adjusted earnings of $0.82 per share, exceeding our internal budget for the quarter and overcoming approximately $0.09 of unfavorable weather." Campbell stated the company remains "on target for the midpoint of full year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance of $3.92 to $4.12 per share." He highlighted strong operational reliability and emphasized regulatory milestones, including the Kansas Corporation Commission's approval of settlement agreements for new natural gas plants and a solar farm, and a unanimous settlement agreement in the Kansas Central rate case.
  • Campbell explained, "We are reaffirming our long-term growth target of 4% to 6% through 2029 based on the 2025 midpoint of $4.02 per share. From 2026 to 2029, we anticipate being in the top half of this guidance range relative to the 2025 baseline with significant additional tailwinds from potential large new customers and investments to serve them."
  • The settlement in the Kansas Central rate case would provide a net revenue increase of $128 million and includes a 9.7% return on equity for transmission delivery charge filings. Surveillance reports will be filed annually to monitor return, with excess earnings above authorized levels being shared 50-50 between customers and the company.
  • Campbell described the economic development pipeline as "expanding 15 gigawatt plus," with a "4 to 6 gigawatt opportunity of new large customer load that represents the most active part of our queue." He noted Panasonic and Meta facilities are ramping up, with additional data center projects in final negotiations.
  • Campbell stated, "We are confident that we'll be successful in winning and serving a large portion of this queue, which will in turn, transform the size and growth of our company."
  • CFO W. Bryan Buckler said, "I'll start by saying this was a quarter of strong execution. And given our results through June, we are firmly on plan and forecasting to be at the midpoint of our $3.92 to $4.12 of adjusted EPS guidance."
  • Buckler noted the company is exiting its Evergy Ventures business with losses of approximately $0.08 in the second quarter, excluded from adjusted earnings, and that cash proceeds from the sale will be used to reduce holding company debt.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed 2025 adjusted EPS guidance range of $3.92 to $4.12 and stated, "with normal weather, the remainder of the year, we believe we are on track to achieve the midpoint of that range."
  • Evergy reiterated its long-term adjusted EPS growth target of 4% to 6% through 2029 and expects to provide an update on its 5-year load forecast, capital and financing plans, and earnings outlook on its year-end call in February.
  • Campbell described anticipated demand growth of 2% to 3% through 2029, with potential to reach 4% to 5% if additional large customer agreements are finalized.

Financial Results

  • Evergy reported second quarter 2025 adjusted earnings of $191 million or $0.82 per share compared to $207 million or $0.90 per share in Q2 2024.
  • Weather normalized demand increased by 1.4% in the second quarter, driven by increases in both residential and commercial usage.
  • Buckler said, "O&M came in on plan in the second quarter, and we expect to come in under budget for O&M for the full year 2025."
  • Losses related to Evergy Ventures investments were approximately $0.08 in the second quarter, excluded from adjusted earnings.

Q&A

  • Nathan Joseph Richardson, Barclays: Asked about timing to derisk equity needs beyond 2025 and potential for forward sales. Campbell responded that there is "no planned equity raise in 2025 and roughly $600 million per year in '26 and '27," with flexibility to address needs gradually via an ATM program.
  • Richardson followed up on Panasonic ramp impacts. Campbell explained that guidance only includes 2% to 3% load growth, and additional customers could bring it to 4% to 5%.
  • Unidentified Analyst (Jefferies): Asked about the balance of the large load customer pipeline and governing factors for addressing the pipeline. Campbell noted it's a balance of customer development timelines and Evergy's ability to serve, emphasizing advanced discussions and significant financial commitments from customers.
  • Analyst also inquired about the earnings review surveillance mechanism. Campbell described it as precedent for the current period but not binding for future cases.
  • Travis Miller, Morningstar: Questioned inclusion of gas plants and solar in 8.5% rate base growth. Campbell confirmed those with predetermination are included, while others are pending.
  • Miller also asked about balancing the system amid dynamic load growth. Campbell stated, "we absolutely believe that we can achieve that balance," emphasizing a robust planning process.
  • Paul Patterson, Glenrock Associates: Asked about the impact of large load tariff proceedings and federal permitting for renewables. Campbell said large loads are advancing work in parallel with tariff proceedings, and for the three approved solar projects, "we believe those 3 products qualify" under current rules.
  • Ryan Michael Levine, Citi: Questioned gas generation buildout labor and EPC strategy. Campbell said Evergy is working with a leading EPC provider and feels good about contractual terms and project execution capabilities.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was constructive but focused on clarifying risk factors, execution timing, and balancing growth versus capital needs. Questions frequently returned to equity requirements, customer pipeline timing, and regulatory approvals.
  • Management maintained a confident and slightly upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A. Phrases such as "we are confident," "we feel good," and "we absolutely believe" were used consistently. There was no significant defensiveness or hesitancy observed.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment this quarter was more focused on execution and risk mitigation, while management's tone remained similarly confident.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 adjusted EPS remained at $3.92 to $4.12 per share, with a reaffirmed long-term EPS growth target of 4% to 6% through 2029.
  • The large customer pipeline expanded, now described as over 15 GW with "4 to 6 gigawatts" actively in play, up from 12.2 GW in Q1. Settlement agreements for new generation in Kansas and Missouri progressed from pending to approved status.
  • Analysts continued to focus on large customer agreements, equity needs, and capital plan flexibility in both quarters, but Q2 featured more detailed questions on project execution and regulatory mechanisms.
  • Management confidence regarding the company's growth trajectory and the flexibility to address equity needs was consistent with the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted the need to appropriately time investment recovery requests relative to load growth.
  • There is ongoing regulatory risk, as some generation projects are still pending approval and federal permitting rules for renewables may evolve.
  • Analysts raised concerns about equity requirements, large customer ramp rates, and the implementation of new tariff mechanisms.
  • Campbell acknowledged the dynamic environment and the importance of balancing new customer load, generation, and infrastructure investments to maintain system reliability.

Final Takeaway

Evergy's management emphasized strong operational execution and regulatory progress in Q2 2025, reaffirmed both near- and long-term EPS guidance, and highlighted an expanding pipeline of large customer projects that could drive significant growth. The company continues to navigate regulatory proceedings and manage capital needs, with flexibility built into its planning. The outlook remains positive, supported by robust customer interest, advancing load growth opportunities, and a clear focus on affordability, reliability, and sustainable investment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: AbCellera signals clinical-stage transition with first patient dosing for ABCL635 and targets mid-2026 data milestone
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AbCellera Biologics Inc. (ABCL) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Carl L.G. Hansen announced, "This quarter, we achieved a major company milestone, receiving Health Canada authorization to initiate AbCellera's first 2 clinical trials for ABCL635 and ABCL575. Today, I'm pleased to announce that dosing has begun in our Phase I clinical trial evaluating ABCL635 for moderate to severe vasomotor symptoms." Hansen described this milestone as the "completion of the transition from a platform company to a clinical stage biotech that we committed to back in 2023."
  • Hansen further stated, "We have added a third program to our pipeline by advancing ABCL688 into IND-enabling studies." He also noted the company's financial strength, reporting approximately $750 million in available liquidity and confirming, "we are well positioned to continue to execute on our strategy and are on track to complete our remaining goals for 2025."
  • CFO Andrew Booth reported, "AbCellera continues to be in a strong liquidity position with approximately $580 million in cash and equivalents and with roughly $170 million in available committed government funding to execute on our strategy."

Outlook

  • Hansen reiterated, "We expect initial safety and efficacy data from this trial in mid-2026." He outlined priorities for the remainder of the year: "executing on our clinical studies with ABCL635 and ABCL575; moving ABCL688 forward in IND-enabling studies; advancing a fourth program from discovery into our pipeline; and finally, bringing our clinical manufacturing capabilities online."
  • The company indicated it is on track to start using its new manufacturing facility at the end of 2025.

Financial Results

  • Booth stated, "Revenue for the quarter was approximately $17 million, comprising of research fees relating to work on partner programs and amounts related to licensing. This compares to revenue of $7 million in the same quarter of 2024."
  • Licensing fees of $10 million were attributed to the Trianni humanized rodent platform, described as a one-time lump sum for the quarter.
  • Research and development expenses for the quarter were approximately $39 million, a $2 million decrease from last year. Sales and marketing expenses were about $3 million, with general and administration expenses at approximately $19 million.
  • The company reported a net loss of roughly $35 million for the quarter, or a loss of $0.12 per share on a basic and diluted basis.
  • Operating activities for the first half of 2025 used approximately $44 million in cash and equivalents. Investments in marketable securities contributed to a net $36 million, mostly in property, plant and equipment.
  • Booth commented, "With over $580 million in cash and equivalents and the unused portion of our secured government funding, we have around $750 million in total available liquidity to execute on our strategy."

Q&A

  • Omotolani A Uthman, Goldman Sachs: Asked about regulatory risks for ABCL635 in light of a delayed PDUFA for elinzanetant and what regulators will focus on. Hansen responded, "Our expectation is that [elinzanetant] would move forward to approval later this year." He added, "We need to demonstrate efficacy... we're excited about the upcoming data and the readout midpoint next year... On the safety side... so far, what has been seen in the class for NK3R antagonist is some liver toxicity... which our antibody does not do. So, we expect that that would not be a concern."
  • Srikripa Devarakonda, Truist: Inquired about trial specifics, enrollment, and market impact of hormone therapy proponents. Hansen detailed, "The single ascending dose will include both menopausal women and healthy men... could be roughly 56, 60 patients or so. Once we progress on to the proof-of-concept, we expect to enroll up to 80 patients." He noted, "Our view has always been that the NK3R class is not in competition with hormone therapies... we would need to only capture a relatively small portion of that market to have this drug be a terrific success."
  • Faisal Ali Khurshid, Leerink Partners: Questioned partnership and licensing revenue sustainability. Booth answered, "This was definitely a one-off payment really related to activities post the acquisition of Trianni... it's not something that we would expect to have happen in the future."
  • Malcolm Hoffman, BMO: Asked about efficacy data for the 635 Phase I study. Hansen explained, "Early in the study, we will be assessing some biomarkers, so LH and FSH in men and women... the real measure of efficacy needs to wait until the POC study... we will be enrolling up to 80 postmenopausal women with moderate to severe VMS."
  • Brendan Mychal Smith, TD Securities: Asked about formulation and dosing. Hansen said, "We believe that we've got an antibody that has a half-life and a potency that would support once monthly dosing on a single subcutaneous dose... that's one of the things we're going to be testing both in the efficacy and in the bioavailability and PK data that will come out of the Phase I study."
  • Joshua Nickerson, Stifel: Asked about U.S. trial sites for 575 and details on 688. Hansen replied, "We are also beginning to engage with the FDA and lay the foundation for the Phase II studies, which you would likely expect to include U.S. sites, but we haven't triggered that yet..." On 688, "I'm afraid we're going to hold this one close to our chest for strategic reasons... our focus right now is getting that to the clinic as quickly as possible."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was generally supportive but focused on regulatory clarity, trial design, market potential, and revenue sustainability, with some probing on risk factors and commercial strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing milestones and progress, and continued a generally positive and forthcoming posture in Q&A, while occasionally holding back specifics for strategic reasons. In both quarters, management used forward-looking language such as "we are well positioned" and "we expect initial safety and efficacy data... in mid-2026."
  • Compared to the prior quarter, the current call had a more confident tone, reflecting tangible clinical progress, while analysts remained focused on risk, trial execution, and commercialization prospects.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter highlighted the transition to a clinical-stage company, with the first patient dosing for ABCL635 and imminent dosing for ABCL575, compared to the previous quarter's focus on preparing for Phase I and pipeline expansion.
  • Revenue increased to $17 million from $4 million, largely due to a one-time licensing fee, while R&D expenses decreased slightly.
  • Management reiterated its confidence in the scientific rationale for ABCL635 and emphasized the importance of near-term data as a value driver, a shift from the previous quarter's focus on establishing clinical trial readiness.
  • Analyst questions in both quarters centered on program risks, trial design, and market opportunity, though the current quarter included more focus on regulatory and commercial milestones.

Risks and Concerns

  • The main scientific risk for ABCL635 remains target engagement, which management stated will be addressed in Phase I through biomarker and proof-of-concept studies.
  • Regulatory focus will be on efficacy and safety, with particular attention to liver toxicity and somnolence, though management expects these not to be issues for ABCL635 due to its design.
  • Management flagged that research fee revenue is expected to trend lower as internal and co-development programs take precedence.
  • Booth clarified that the $10 million licensing fee is a one-off event and not expected to recur.

Final Takeaway

AbCellera's Q2 2025 call signals a pivotal transition to a clinical-stage biotech, marked by the initiation of its first patient dosing for ABCL635 and the advancement of ABCL575 and ABCL688 in the pipeline. The company remains focused on achieving key clinical milestones, building out its manufacturing capabilities, and managing liquidity effectively. Management expressed strong conviction in the scientific and commercial prospects of its lead programs, with mid-2026 set as the anticipated timeframe for initial clinical data readouts that could shape the company's future trajectory.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nu Skin targets $1.48B–$1.55B revenue and $3.05–$3.25 EPS for 2025 as Prysm iO rollout and India market prep advance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Nu Skin Enterprises (NUS) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Ryan S. Napierski, President and CEO, reported that "we delivered revenue at the high end of our guidance range and significantly exceeded our earnings per share forecast for the second quarter." He highlighted revenue of $386.1 million and earnings per share of $0.43, attributing the strong earnings to "disciplined approach to cost management and operational efficiency improvements."
  • Napierski detailed that Latin America experienced "strong year-over-year growth" as part of the developing market strategy, while North America saw declines due to "increasing macro pressures." Japan showed growth with its subscription-based wellness business, and the Rhyz segment's manufacturing grew 17% year-over-year.
  • The CEO announced the upcoming launch of Prysm iO, describing it as "our truly intelligent wellness platform" built on "more than 20 years of selective scientific research and development" and leveraging AI. The rollout will start in limited quantities for qualified sales leaders in Q4 2025, with broader launches in 2026.
  • Napierski indicated the company is preparing for entry into India, with a "Q4 premarket opening for qualified India eligible sales leaders" and a formal launch anticipated in mid-2026, using a localized product portfolio and digital-first infrastructure.
  • Project Accelerate, an initiative focused on operational efficiency, has driven "significant improvements in our Q2 operating margin to 8%."
  • The CEO emphasized balance sheet strength, noting "we have also strengthened our balance sheet to become a cash to debt positive."
  • James D. Thomas, CFO, stated, "earnings per share came in at $0.43. This surpassed our guidance by $0.13 and demonstrated significant improvement over the prior year $0.21 adjusted earnings per share due to our cost efficiency efforts."

Outlook

  • The company projects third quarter revenue between $360 million and $390 million, with earnings per share anticipated in the range of $0.25 to $0.35.
  • For the full year 2025, the company projects revenue of $1.48 billion to $1.55 billion and earnings per share of $3.05 to $3.25, with adjusted earnings per share of $1.15 to $1.35.
  • Management flagged ongoing "global uncertainties, including potential tariff impacts and evolving geopolitical conditions," and noted a "disciplined and measured approach by narrowing our revenue outlook for the back half of 2025 and increasing our earnings per share."

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q2 2025 was $386.1 million, with neutral foreign currency impact.
  • Earnings per share for Q2 2025 was $0.43.
  • Gross margin for Q2 was 68.8%, compared to 70% in the prior year; core Nu Skin business gross margin was 77.5%, up 140 basis points from the prior year.
  • Selling expense as a percentage of revenue was 33.2%; within the core Nu Skin segment, selling expense was 40%.
  • General and administrative expenses were down $11.2 million compared to Q2 2024.
  • Operating margin for the quarter was 8%, up 260 basis points from adjusted operating margin of 5.4% in the prior year.
  • Cash flow from operations for the quarter was $35.8 million, with $264 million in cash at quarter-end. The company achieved a net cash positive position ahead of schedule.
  • Approximately $3 million was returned to shareholders via dividend; no stock repurchases occurred during the quarter.

Q&A

  • David Joseph Storms, Stonegate Capital Partners, asked about initiatives or geographies that could influence the guidance range. Napierski responded that "Latin America overperforming expectations, a little bit offset through North America... Korea, we're seeing improving trends there. China is always a big question mark, especially with the macro uncertainty there economically and just geopolitically."
  • Storms inquired about Latin America's strength and runway. Napierski explained that a simplified and scalable model, local product focus, and healthy retail profit for sellers are driving the region's performance, with Prysm iO expected to further boost growth.
  • Storms asked about continued cost optimization. Thomas highlighted "4 consecutive quarters of sequential growth in gross margin," ongoing inventory management, and efforts to replace physical infrastructure with technology to drive further margin improvement.
  • On capital allocation, Napierski prioritized "investing in growth of the business," particularly Prysm iO and developing markets like India, with Thomas reiterating the company's focus on funding growth, servicing debt, paying dividends, and opportunistic share repurchases.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions focused on guidance sensitivity, growth in Latin America, margin improvement, and capital allocation, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, seeking clarity but expressing appreciation for management's transparency.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, using phrases such as "we feel confident in our ability to navigate our forward" and "we remain confident in our ability to adapt."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was notably more confident, citing operational improvements and strong cash flow, while analysts maintained a constructive, slightly optimistic approach.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q2 saw revenue at the high end of guidance and earnings per share significantly above forecast, versus Q1 where revenue was at the top end of guidance and adjusted EPS exceeded expectations.
  • Margin expansion and operational efficiency improvements were more strongly emphasized in Q2, with Project Accelerate highlighted as a key driver.
  • Guidance for the remainder of 2025 is more specific, with revenue and earnings per share targets provided, compared to a more cautious and broad outlook in Q1.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward Prysm iO rollout and India market entry, while continuing the developing market strategy and operational improvements highlighted in Q1.
  • Management's tone in Q2 was more assertive about future prospects and operational discipline, while analyst focus remained on market performance and margin drivers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "ongoing global uncertainties, including potential tariff impacts and evolving geopolitical conditions."
  • North America and China remain areas of macroeconomic concern, with China described as "always a big question mark."
  • The company is taking a "disciplined and measured approach" to guidance, reflecting a cautious outlook amid external volatility.

Final Takeaway

Nu Skin Enterprises delivered Q2 revenue and earnings per share at the high end of guidance, supported by margin expansion, disciplined cost management, and strong operational cash flow. Management outlined a focused strategy for the second half of 2025, prioritizing the global launch of Prysm iO and expansion into India, while maintaining a cautious approach to guidance due to continued macroeconomic uncertainty. The company’s progress in developing markets and ongoing operational improvements position it to pursue growth and return value to shareholders as it prepares for major product and market launches in 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Xeris raises 2025 revenue guidance to $280M–$290M following 49% Q2 growth, led by Recorlev
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Xeris Biopharma Holdings, Inc. (XERS) Q2 2025

Management View

  • John P. Shannon, CEO, reported "the strong momentum we established in the first quarter continued into the second quarter, driving another period of incredible performance and positioning us to outperform our original expectations for 2025." He highlighted total revenue growth of 49% to almost $72 million, with commercial product portfolio revenue growing 46% to approximately $68 million due to increased patient demand across all three products.
  • Recorlev led growth, increasing 136% year-over-year to over $31 million in Q2. Shannon said, "Recorlev continues to lead our growth with very strong demand driven by its differentiation as a much-needed treatment for patients with hypercortisolemia and endogenous Cushing's syndrome."
  • Gvoke posted Q2 revenue of $23 million, up 17% year-over-year, with prescription volume continuing to trend upward. Shannon explained, "Gvoke is well positioned for steady long-term growth for many years to come."
  • Keveyis achieved more than $11 million in revenue, with a modest increase in average patients on therapy and growth in new patient starts, reinforcing its role for primary periodic paralysis.
  • Shannon announced the company is raising its full-year revenue guidance to $280 million to $290 million, "surpassing the high end of our previous guidance and representing year-over-year growth of 40% at the midpoint."
  • Shannon discussed the Analyst and Investor Day, emphasizing long-term vision and key growth drivers, especially Recorlev and pipeline candidate XP-8121, which targets patients with hypothyroidism who cannot achieve control with daily oral levothyroxine.
  • CFO Steven M. Pieper said, "We had another record-breaking quarter. On a year-over-year basis, total revenue grew 49% to $71.5 million, while net product revenue increased 46% to $67.7 million." Pieper added, "Adjusted EBITDA in the quarter was a positive $12.5 million... This further demonstrates our ability to translate strong top line performance into meaningful bottom line results."

Outlook

  • The company revised its 2025 full-year revenue guidance to $280 million to $290 million from the prior range of $260 million to $275 million, reflecting strong year-to-date results and confidence in sustained demand, particularly for Recorlev.
  • Pieper stated, "We continue to expect a modest improvement in gross margin compared to 2024." The company now anticipates a low to mid-teens percentage increase in SG&A and R&D expenses year-over-year, compared to prior expectations of mid- to high single-digit increases.
  • Pieper emphasized, "We remain committed to delivering continued positive adjusted EBITDA going forward."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q2 was $71.5 million, with net product revenue at $67.7 million.
  • Recorlev net revenue reached $31.4 million, an increase of 136% year-over-year and a sequential increase of $5.9 million.
  • Gvoke net revenue was $23.5 million, up 17% year-over-year, with total Gvoke prescriptions growing 5% and benefiting from gross to net favorability.
  • Keveyis net revenue was $11.5 million, with a slight uptick from Q1 2025 and modest increases in patients on therapy and new starts.
  • Royalty contract and other revenue totaled $3.8 million, primarily from a one-time milestone payment related to Gvoke VialDx.
  • Gross margin was 82%, slightly below Q1 due to nonroutine Gvoke capacity expansion costs. Year-to-date gross margin was 84%.
  • R&D expenses were $8.1 million, up $2.2 million from last year, mainly due to investments in XP-8121 and products. SG&A expenses were $44.4 million, up 11% year-over-year, but flat sequentially.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $12.5 million, an improvement of nearly $13 million over the prior year.

Q&A

  • Chase Richard Knickerbocker, Craig-Hallum: Asked about Gvoke gross to net benefit and sustainability. Pieper responded, "We anticipate that favorability to continue for the balance of the year."
  • Knickerbocker: Asked about Gvoke script growth outlook. Shannon explained, "The third quarter is always a bigger growth quarter with the back-to-school movement... I think you're going to see an increase coming up."
  • Knickerbocker: Inquired on Recorlev prescription sources and average dosage. Shannon shared, "It's pretty much endo. And at least half of our patients are new to therapy... we don't think we're going to see real material movement in the dose range, at least for some time."
  • Knickerbocker: Asked about competitive dynamics if a new drug launches. Shannon stated, "Another player in this space brings more noise to that situation, and I think that's good for all of us."
  • Alexandra Doering von Riesemann, Piper Sandler: Asked about long-term spend and expansion for Recorlev. Shannon indicated that investments and commercial footprint expansion are ongoing and will continue as the market grows.
  • Christian J. Clark, Leerink Partners: Asked about plans for additional Recorlev clinical data. Shannon replied, "Yes, and we'll continue to generate more and more data around that."
  • Jason Dorr, Oppenheimer: Asked about Gvoke market evolution and VialDx revenue outlook. Shannon discussed long-term market opportunity and noted partner American Regent will launch VialDx by year-end, with more guidance expected in the future.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability of Gvoke's gross to net benefit, prescription growth, Recorlev's physician adoption, and competition, reflecting a slightly positive to neutral sentiment with probing but not confrontational tone.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, emphasizing sustained momentum, robust growth, and strategic investments. Pieper stated, "We are well positioned to realize the full potential of our portfolio and pipeline."
  • Compared to Q1, both management and analysts sustained a positive sentiment, with management slightly more assertive about growth opportunities and strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance increased from a range of $260 million–$275 million in Q1 to $280 million–$290 million in Q2, with year-over-year growth at midpoint rising from nearly 32% to 40%.
  • Recorlev continued to be the primary driver, with net revenue rising from $25.5 million to $31.4 million quarter-over-quarter.
  • Gvoke and Keveyis posted consistent growth, with Gvoke revenue increasing from $20.8 million to $23.5 million and Keveyis remaining stable.
  • Management shifted guidance on SG&A and R&D expense increases from mid- to high single-digit to low to mid-teens percentage, reflecting greater investment in growth priorities.
  • Analyst questions remained focused on sustainability, competition, and product-specific trends, while management's confidence appeared even stronger versus the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged increased investment in SG&A and R&D, stating these are deliberate to support growth and the upcoming XP-8121 pivotal trial.
  • A potential new competitor in the hypercortisolism market was referenced, but management indicated this could expand market awareness rather than create risk.
  • No immediate concerns were raised about supply chain, manufacturing, or current competitive threats.

Final Takeaway

Xeris Biopharma's second quarter demonstrated robust momentum across its commercial portfolio, led by substantial growth in Recorlev and solid contributions from Gvoke and Keveyis. Management raised full-year revenue guidance to $280 million–$290 million, reflecting confidence in underlying demand and strategic investments. The company reiterated its commitment to operational discipline, positive adjusted EBITDA, and targeted pipeline expansion, positioning itself for continued growth and value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Akebia outlines access to over 275,000 dialysis patients for Vafseo in Q4 as DaVita pilot launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Akebia Therapeutics (AKBA) Q2 2025

Management View

  • John P. Butler, CEO, reported that "we generated over $13 million in Vafseo revenue in Q2 with approximately $12 million in demand sales, a 55% increase over Q1." Butler highlighted that access to Vafseo is set to expand, stating "as of September, we expect the physicians of these dialysis organizations will be able to write a prescription for Vafseo without restriction, bringing the total patients with access to over 75,000." He revealed that DaVita is preparing an operational pilot for Vafseo, with initial orders placed and patients expected to receive the drug in mid-August, paving the way for access to "at least 275,000 patients later in Q4."
  • Butler also announced the full enrollment of the VOICE trial, with over 2,100 patients, and the initiation of the VOCAL study at DaVita facilities, targeting 350 patients to examine red blood cell characteristics.
  • Regarding label expansion, Butler stated, "We completed a Type B meeting with the FDA in May" and are planning for an "active ESA comparator" in the upcoming VALOR trial for nondialysis CKD patients.
  • Erik John Ostrowski, CFO, stated, "Total revenues...were $62.5 million this quarter as compared to $43.6 million in Q2 of last year representing an increase of $18.9 million." Ostrowski added, "We generated $247,000 of net income this quarter as compared to a net loss of $8.6 million in Q2 of last year." He noted a decrease in cost of goods sold to $9.9 million and ended Q2 with $137.3 million in cash and cash equivalents, emphasizing, "We believe our existing cash resources and the cash we expect to generate...are sufficient to fund our current operating plan to profitability."

Outlook

  • Management expects prescribing access for Vafseo to increase from approximately 40,000 patients to over 75,000 in the third quarter, with the DaVita pilot enabling access for over 275,000 patients in the fourth quarter.
  • The VOICE study will complete in late 2026 with data available in early 2027, and the VALOR trial is planned for initiation by the end of the year, targeting nondialysis CKD patients.
  • Butler stated, "We're working to initiate VALOR by the end of the year."

Financial Results

  • Total revenues were $62.5 million, with net product revenues at $60.5 million driven by Vafseo and Auryxia.
  • Vafseo sales reached $13.3 million, while Auryxia sales were $47.2 million. Cost of goods sold declined to $9.9 million, and R&D expenses increased to $11 million, attributed to Vafseo clinical trials.
  • Net income was $247,000, compared to a net loss of $8.6 million in the same quarter last year.
  • Ostrowski explained, "This quarter's net income was driven by the increase in revenues, which was partially offset by $5.4 million of interest expense...as well as $7 million in noncash expense related to the change in fair value of our warrant liability."

Q&A

  • Roger Song, Jefferies: Asked about patient segments and payer mix changes with broader access and DaVita's onboarding. Nicholas Grund responded that "home patients...being about 12% of the total scripts and the remainder being for in-center patients," and expects "continued broad uses" as DaVita and others come online. Grund noted "about 20% of total prescriptions being filled in the Medicare Advantage segment...where 80% is Medicare fee-for-service," but this distribution is shifting as new contracts are implemented.
  • Song inquired about average dose strength. Grund replied, "we're seeing refill scripts being at about a 28% increase over the 300-milligram starting dose."
  • Julian Reed Harrison, BTIG: Asked about DaVita's pilot timeline and protocol implementation at other large dialysis organizations. Butler said, "the DaVita operational pilot...will last 3 months," with network-wide access expected by "the middle of November." Regarding Fresenius, Butler stated, "We have not been able to progress yet...they would probably do a similar kind of operational pilot before they get broad access."
  • Harrison asked about the VALOR trial timeline. Butler said, "the numbers we've been giving in the past about a 1,500-patient trial...it's really all about how quickly we can enroll."
  • Mazahir Lukman Alimohamed, Leerink Partners: Asked about Auryxia's resilience and generic competition. Grund explained that increased access post-bundle is driving Auryxia's use. Ostrowski noted, "new generic competition could come up at any time. So we budget conservatively."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally positive, focusing on launch metrics, access expansion, and market segmentation, but pressed for details on payer mix, dose trends, and competitive dynamics.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently citing strong progress and growth. Butler stated, "we're happy with the progress on growing breadth and depth of prescribing, increasing patient access and physician education."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analyst and management tone showed increased optimism, with analysts more focused on access expansion and management providing concrete timelines and growth metrics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language sharpened to specify timelines and patient access milestones, shifting from anticipation of LDO pilots to confirmation of DaVita's operational pilot and broader access.
  • Strategic focus evolved from early-stage launch to scaling access and execution of pivotal trials (VOICE fully enrolled, VOCAL initiated, VALOR planning advanced).
  • Analysts' focus moved from initial launch and reimbursement to specifics of patient mix, dosing, and market share as large providers onboard.
  • Key financials improved, with total revenues and profitability higher than Q1, and cash resources strengthening.
  • Management's confidence increased, supported by tangible sales growth and expanded prescribing access.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified uncertainty regarding future Auryxia sales due to potential additional generic competition. Ostrowski cautioned, "future Auryxia sales levels are challenging to predict due to the uncertainty around the timing of potential additional generic competition."
  • The company noted that Vafseo sales in the quarter were derived from prelaunch inventory, which may not reflect ongoing manufacturing costs.
  • Analysts raised questions about payer mix evolution and coverage for Vafseo, as well as competition dynamics for Auryxia.

Final Takeaway

Akebia Therapeutics emphasized accelerating Vafseo's expansion in the dialysis market, with prescribing access expected to more than triple in the coming quarters as DaVita's pilot concludes and other large providers come online. Strong revenue growth, successful trial progress, and a solid cash position underpin management's confidence in advancing Vafseo toward standard of care status and pursuing label expansion, while remaining vigilant regarding generic competition for Auryxia and evolving reimbursement landscapes.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Take-Two raises FY26 net bookings outlook to $6.15B as mobile and NBA 2K drive growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (TTWO) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Strauss H. Zelnick, Executive Chairman & CEO, opened the call by stating fiscal 2026 is off to an excellent start, citing "ongoing demand for our core franchises and the increasingly diversified successful nature of our business." Zelnick highlighted that "net bookings for the first quarter exceeded $1.4 billion, which was meaningfully above the high end of our expectations, led by the outperformance of several Mobile titles as well as the continued success of NBA 2K and the Grand Theft Auto series."
  • Zelnick announced an increased net bookings outlook for fiscal 2026 to a range of $6.05 billion to $6.15 billion, expressing confidence in the long-term pipeline and stating the company expects "to achieve record levels of net bookings in fiscal '27 that we believe will establish a higher baseline for our business and set us on a path of enhanced profitability."
  • Key product performance updates included Toon Blast's 22% growth over last year, Match Factory!'s 33% increase, and Color Block Jam becoming "the highest grossing title in the studio's history." Engagement for Grand Theft Auto Online benefited from "the record setting launch of Grand Theft Auto VI Trailer Two and the successful release of the Money Fronts Summer Content Pack," with new player accounts for GTA Online up over 50% year-over-year. NBA 2K25 reached over 11.5 million units sold, with daily active users and MyCAREER daily active users both up 30% and recurrent consumer spending up 48%.
  • Karl Slatoff, President, previewed upcoming releases including Mafia: The Old Country, NBA 2K26, and Borderlands 4, and highlighted "more than 2.5 million wishlists across all platforms" for Mafia: The Old Country. Slatoff characterized this as "one of the strongest lineups of 2K's history."
  • Lainie Goldstein, CFO, stated "We delivered first quarter net bookings of $1.42 billion, which was significantly above our guidance range of $1.25 billion to $1.3 billion. This reflected better-than-expected performance from several Mobile titles, including Toon Blast, Match Factory!, NBA 2K All-Star and Color Block Jam, as well as NBA 2K and the Grand Theft Auto series."

Outlook

  • Goldstein raised the net bookings outlook range to $6.05 billion to $6.15 billion, representing 8% growth over fiscal 2025 at the midpoint, with the largest contributors expected to be NBA 2K, the Grand Theft Auto series, Toon Blast, Borderlands 4, Match Factory!, the Red Dead Redemption series, Color Block Jam, Empires & Puzzles, and Words With Friends.
  • Recurrent consumer spending is now expected to grow approximately 4%, revised upward from the prior forecast of flat, and to represent 76% of net bookings. NBA 2K is expected to grow mid-teens, Mobile to grow low single digits, and Grand Theft Auto Online to decline.
  • Operating cash flow is expected to be approximately $130 million, and capital expenditures approximately $140 million. GAAP net revenue guidance is $6.1 billion to $6.2 billion. Operating expenses are forecasted at $3.84 billion to $3.86 billion.
  • For Q2, net bookings are projected at $1.7 billion to $1.75 billion, with operating expenses planned at $1.02 billion to $1.03 billion.

Financial Results

  • First quarter net bookings were $1.42 billion. GAAP net revenue increased 12% to $1.5 billion. Cost of revenue declined 1% to $559 million, and operating expenses decreased 3% to $923 million.
  • Recurrent consumer spending grew 17% for the period, accounting for 83% of net bookings. NBA 2K grew nearly 50%, Mobile grew low teens, and Grand Theft Auto Online increased low single digits.
  • On a management basis, operating expenses rose 3% year-over-year, slightly above the forecast due to higher personnel costs. Marketing expenses were within the forecast range.

Q&A

  • Eric Owen Handler, MKM Partners: Asked about NBA 2K's growth drivers, noting a 48% rise in recurrent consumer spending. Karl Slatoff explained, "It's really both of our primary RCS generating modes, which is MyCAREER and also -- MyCAREER and our -- MyTEAM...we're getting much, much better about managing and reading our in-game telemetry, knowing what the consumers like to spend money on, what they like to engage with."
  • Douglas Lippl Creutz, TD Cowen: Inquired about advertising revenue trends. Strauss Zelnick responded, "we adjusted our approach to advertising by moving from hyper-casual to hybrid-casual...we feel very good about sort of where we're at, and we would certainly hope to grow from here."
  • Christopher Joseph Schoell, UBS: Questioned Mobile segment growth and guidance. Lainie Goldstein replied, "We do see some mature titles that have achieved outstanding results on a multiyear basis...given the age of these titles, we really still expect moderation."
  • Andrew Jordan Marok, Raymond James: Asked about game pricing for Borderlands and broader pricing environment. Zelnick stated, "We believe that any consumer experience is the intersection of the thing itself and what you paid for the thing. So our goal is to vastly exceed expectations."
  • Michael Joseph Hickey, The Benchmark Company: Queried about macroeconomic impacts. Zelnick offered, "I do think that there is some risk...consumers are going to be a little more careful with their spending...they're going to go to quality."
  • Zhihua Yang, Oppenheimer: Asked about platform strategy and Roblox. Zelnick clarified, "Roblox is aimed at kids largely. And not all of our games are rated E...not all of our games would work on Roblox, whether that's work technically, work artistically or work commercially."
  • Clayton Keever Griffin, MoffettNathanson: Inquired about court rulings and app store strategies. Zelnick said, "We're going to continue to cooperate with all of the app stores. They are really important partners...the movement is all in the same direction, and that direction is what's good for the consumer."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about sustainability of mobile growth, advertising revenue recovery, macroeconomic risks, and pricing strategy, reflecting a neutral to cautiously positive sentiment with a focus on consistency and risk management.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone during prepared remarks and Q&A. Zelnick emphasized, "We strive daily to do our best work...to make the biggest and best hits in the entertainment business," and repeatedly underlined confidence in the pipeline and strategy.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management retained their generally positive tone, though analysts pressed more on macro concerns and sustainability of recent outperformance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw an increase in net bookings guidance and a positive revision to recurrent consumer spending, compared to the previous quarter's expectation of flat growth in this metric.
  • Fiscal 2026 guidance was raised from $5.9 billion-$6 billion to $6.05 billion-$6.15 billion, and recurrent consumer spending target moved from flat to 4% growth.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from specific questions about impairment and margin sustainability in Q4 to questions about the durability of mobile outperformance, advertising recovery, and platform strategy in Q1.
  • Management’s tone remained confident; however, there was additional emphasis on mobile strength and new product launches.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted potential moderation in mobile growth due to maturity of certain titles.
  • Macro environment uncertainty and consumer spending caution were acknowledged by Zelnick.
  • Analysts noted concerns about advertising revenue trends and the sustainability of mobile performance.
  • No new material risks related to product development or pipeline were disclosed.

Final Takeaway

Take-Two Interactive entered fiscal 2026 with strong momentum, raising its net bookings outlook and reporting robust growth across core franchises and mobile titles. Management remains confident in the company’s pipeline and ability to deliver shareholder returns, supported by successful product launches, higher recurrent consumer spending, and a disciplined approach to capital allocation. Focus remains on quality, engagement, and expanding the reach of key titles across platforms.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Akamai outlines 40%–45% cloud infrastructure ARR growth target while expanding AI security and compute portfolio
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO F. Thomson Leighton reported that "Akamai had an excellent second quarter with results coming in above our guidance for revenue, margin and earnings per share." He highlighted revenue growth to $1.043 billion, a 7% increase year-over-year, and noted that non-GAAP earnings per share reached $1.73, up 9% year-over-year and $0.15 above the high end of guidance.
  • Leighton emphasized the stabilization of delivery product line revenue and the "solid growth of our security and compute product lines," with significant opportunities in AI. He stated that cloud infrastructure services (CIS) revenue was $71 million, growing 30% year-over-year, and projected "even faster growth throughout the remainder of the year as we start recognizing revenue from some large deals signed earlier this year."
  • Leighton introduced a new AI gateway solution, created to address challenges with deploying large language models, making AI "faster, more secure and significantly more affordable." He described recent customer contracts, including a 3-year $16 million renewal with an AI leader, a 2-year $28 million deal with a leading travel company, and other major multi-year agreements spanning the globe.
  • Security solutions, notably Guardicore Segmentation, continued to see strong demand due to ransomware risks, with Leighton citing multiple high-value wins in financial services, insurance, and manufacturing. Akamai's segmentation solution was recognized as Customer Favorite in the Forrester Wave: Zero Trust Platforms report.
  • Leighton welcomed two new directors to Akamai's Board: Janaki Akella and Bas Burger, highlighting their expertise in cloud computing, cybersecurity, and global business leadership.
  • CFO Edward J. McGowan stated, "we delivered very solid second quarter results with total Q2 revenue of $1.043 billion, which was up 7% year-over-year as reported and 6% in constant currency." McGowan noted a "combination of higher-than-expected revenue, lower-than-expected bandwidth costs, higher interest income related to the convertible debt issuance in May and lower share count as a result of our stock buyback activity" as key drivers for EPS performance. He disclosed a one-time $5 million positive benefit to gross margin from bandwidth and colocation credits in Q2.

Outlook

  • For Q3, Akamai projects revenue in the range of $1.035 billion to $1.050 billion, representing 3% to 4% growth as reported, and non-GAAP EPS guidance of $1.62 to $1.66. Full-year 2025 revenue is expected between $4.135 billion and $4.205 billion, with non-GAAP EPS projected at $6.60 to $6.80.
  • McGowan stated, "we continue to expect CIS ARR year-over-year growth in the range of 40% to 45% in constant currency at the end of the year" and security revenue growth of approximately 10% in constant currency for 2025. The combined ARR for Zero Trust enterprise and API security solutions is expected to increase by 30% to 35%. CapEx for 2025 is anticipated at approximately 20% of total revenue.

Financial Results

  • Compute revenue reached $171 million, up 13% year-over-year. CIS revenue was $71 million, up 30% year-over-year. Other cloud applications revenue totaled $100 million, up 4% year-over-year.
  • Security revenue climbed to $552 million, up 11% year-over-year, with API security and Zero Trust solutions generating $67 million, up 48% year-over-year. Delivery revenue was $320 million, down 3% year-over-year, but described as "well above our expectations."
  • International revenue was $516 million, up 10% year-over-year, accounting for 49% of total revenue.
  • Non-GAAP net income in Q2 was $251 million. CapEx was $214 million, or 21% of revenue. The company spent $300 million on stock buybacks during the quarter, with $1.2 billion remaining on its repurchase authorization.

Q&A

  • Michael Joseph Cikos, Needham & Company: Asked about compute revenue finishing below the targeted 15%. CEO Leighton explained the timing of revenue recognition for large CIS deals could impact the full-year compute total but forecasted 40%–45% ARR growth.
  • Lawrence Vensko, Guggenheim: Inquired about the delivery business and sustainability of improvements. Leighton described a changed competitive landscape, stating "4 of our biggest competitors pre-pandemic are gone," contributing to improved pricing and traffic trends.
  • Mark (for Fatima Balani, Citi): Queried CIS contract structure and commitments. CFO McGowan confirmed large contracts have minimum commitments and ramps, supporting high confidence in second-half growth.
  • Frank Garrett Louthan, Raymond James: Asked about guidance adjustments from including TikTok revenue and cloud application trends. McGowan said TikTok adds about $40 million to $50 million in U.S. revenue, but guidance midpoint is "up much more than that," citing broad strength.
  • Jackson Edmund Ader, KeyBanc: Sought detail on CIS contract visibility and go-to-market investments. McGowan described customer-specific ramp dynamics and a growing CIS pipeline, with strong API security demand.
  • Jonathan Frank Ho, William Blair: Asked about API/micro-segmentation growth and overall security pace. Leighton cited "30% to 35% ARR growth" for these products, while legacy products grow slower. Leighton also highlighted Akamai's unique new AI firewall.
  • Jonathan Eisenson, Morgan Stanley: Asked about Edgio revenue contribution. McGowan said it is difficult to separate, but overall acquisition impact is "tracking just as we had hoped."
  • Daniel Uriah Hibshman, Craig-Hallum: Asked about delivery revenue drivers. McGowan pointed to healthy traffic growth, notably in video and software downloads, and moderating price declines.
  • Andres Bernabe Miranda Lopez, D.A. Davidson: Asked about compute revenue headwinds from legacy transfers. McGowan said the impact was minimal.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on drivers of compute growth, delivery stabilization, and contract visibility, reflecting a slightly positive to neutral tone, with particular interest in the sustainability of recent trends and execution on large contracts.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly citing "very high confidence" in contract ramps and pipeline growth, and using phrases such as "we feel very confident," and "we're very encouraged."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistently confident, while analysts were more focused on the timing and sustainability of growth, shifting from cautious optimism to more specific questions about execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated from 3% year-over-year in Q1 to 7% in Q2. CIS growth increased from 14% to 30% year-over-year. Security revenue growth also improved, with API and Zero Trust solutions growing 48% in Q2.
  • Management raised full-year revenue and EPS guidance, now including TikTok revenue, and outlined higher projected CIS ARR growth.
  • Analysts in Q2 pressed for details on large contract execution and delivery trends, while in Q1, questions focused more on product mix and early signs of stabilization.
  • Management's tone in both quarters was confident, but Q2 featured more emphasis on new AI solutions and expanding board expertise.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited increased colocation and related costs in Q3 as additional compute capacity comes online, impacting gross margin.
  • Guidance indicates that qualified compute partner sales could lower overall gross margin by approximately 70 basis points in 2025.
  • There is some timing risk to compute revenue recognition from large contracts, depending on customer ramp-up schedules. McGowan stated "if it's a month or 2 late in terms of when the customers ramp up, that may potentially land revenue slightly below 15%."
  • Management noted ongoing investments in go-to-market and sales capacity may take time to deliver incremental results, likely resulting in lower operating margins in the second half of the year.

Final Takeaway

Akamai Technologies reported strong Q2 results with revenue, margin, and EPS all coming in above guidance. The company is seeing accelerating growth in its cloud infrastructure and security businesses, supported by large new contracts and an expanding product portfolio, particularly in AI and API security. Management expects continued robust growth in cloud ARR and security, with a healthy pipeline for the second half of the year, while acknowledging some timing and margin headwinds as new investments and contract ramps play out. The addition of new board members with deep expertise in AI and cloud positions Akamai to capitalize on ongoing market opportunities and evolving customer needs.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Onto Innovation anticipates 50% AI packaging revenue surge in Q4 2025 while advancing Semilab acquisition
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Onto Innovation Inc. (ONTO) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Michael P. Plisinski reported a strong second quarter with revenue and operating margin both exceeding the midpoint of guidance, highlighting "excellent progress in new product adoption" and announcing a strategic agreement to acquire several synergistic product lines from Semilab International. Plisinski stated, "The acquired product lines are projected to add over $130 million in annual revenue, be immediately accretive to both gross and operating margin and increase first year earnings per share by more than 10% with an implied price to EBITDA ratio of 10x."
  • Regarding product performance, Plisinski emphasized the next-generation Dragonfly platform's milestone: "Our next-generation Dragonfly platform achieved a significant milestone in the quarter when we successfully validated the platform's optical performance and scan time against our key customers' comprehensive new requirements for 2.5D logic packaging. Our Dragonfly platform performed exceptionally well, passing all tests and customer pull remains strong."
  • He also noted, "We now expect to ship next gen Dragonfly systems to several customers in the second half of the year" and projected a "sharp acceleration in AI logic packaging revenue in the fourth quarter, with revenue increasing at least 50% quarter-over-quarter."
  • CFO Brian K. Roberts stated, "We delivered a solid second quarter with revenue of $253.6 million or an increase of about 5% year-over-year. Gross margin for the second quarter was 54.5%. Excluding approximately $1.1 million of expense incurred in the quarter due to tariffs, gross margin would have been approximately 55%. Operating margin of 25.9% was near the high end of our expected range and was a result of productivity gains in R&D and operations."
  • Roberts highlighted the pending Semilab acquisition: "Once the acquisition closes, which is expected to happen in the coming months, we will pay Semilab $475 million in cash and issued 706,215 shares of our common stock."

Outlook

  • Revenue for the third quarter is expected to be in the range of $210 million to $225 million, reflecting an anticipated slowdown in advanced node spending. Plisinski reiterated, "We are expecting a sharp acceleration in AI packaging spend in the fourth quarter, which gives us confidence that Q4 revenue will return to a level more consistent with what we reported in the first and second quarters of 2025."
  • Gross margin in the third quarter is anticipated to be between 53% and 55%, including a 1 percentage point impact due to tariffs.
  • Operating margin for Q3 is projected at 18% to 21% due to lower revenue, but management expects a return to previous levels in Q4 alongside the revenue rebound.
  • Earnings per share for Q3 is guided in the range of $0.75 to $0.95, assuming a tax rate of approximately 15% and 49 million shares outstanding.
  • The acquisition of Semilab is expected to be immediately accretive to margins and EPS, not including future synergies.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for the second quarter reached $253.6 million, with gross margin at 54.5% and operating margin at 25.9%.
  • Earnings per share for the quarter was $1.25, with FX losses and tax impacting results by $0.01 and $0.02, respectively.
  • Advanced nodes revenue was $89 million, specialty devices and advanced packaging revenue was $117 million, and software and services revenue was $48 million.
  • Cash from operations was $58 million, and the company ended the quarter with $895 million in cash and investments.

Q&A

  • Craig Andrew Ellis, B. Riley Securities: Asked for details on Dragonfly milestones and customer traction. Plisinski responded that passing customer tests was significant and said, "We're still solidifying the system, making sure it performs as it is now over months of testing... so that we'll be ready to ship a high-quality product on time."
  • Ellis also inquired about Q4 revenue drivers. Plisinski explained, "The range is less driven by the programs and more by the customer demands... the real variables are in the customer demand."
  • Matthew Patrick Prisco, Cantor Fitzgerald: Sought clarification on Dragonfly shipments and growth drivers for 2026. Plisinski confirmed intentions to ship tools this year and sees Dragonfly and films as top growth drivers for next year.
  • Brian Edward Chin, Stifel: Probed whether subsurface defect inspection in Q4 is mainly for one customer. Plisinski clarified, "It's actually not one customer. It's several customers... we continue to find new applications."
  • Hoonshik Yang, Oppenheimer: Asked about Semilab growth rates. Plisinski indicated expectations for above-average growth and noted ongoing work to finalize integration synergies.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on product validation, drivers of Q4 rebound, and acquisition strategy, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on execution and competitive positioning.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, highlighting "excellent progress" and "strong demand," but was more measured during Q&A, often referencing customer-driven demand variability and ongoing integration efforts. The phrase "we are confident" was used regarding Q4 rebound.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone has shifted from cautious optimism to increased confidence about fourth quarter prospects, while analysts remain focused on execution and upside potential tied to AI packaging and Semilab integration.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q3 remains lower than Q2, consistent with prior expectations of a pause in advanced node spending, but the outlook for Q4 is now more positive, with management expecting AI logic packaging revenue to increase at least 50% quarter-over-quarter.
  • The Q2 call introduced the Semilab acquisition as a strategic step, compared to last quarter's manufacturing expansion focus.
  • Management's confidence in portfolio progress and market share expansion is higher, emphasizing new product validation and customer wins, while analysts continue to probe for clarity on timing and integration.
  • Financially, revenue and EPS declined sequentially from Q1 to Q2, but the outlook for Q4 is more optimistic than previously communicated.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff exposure remains a challenge, with management stating, "We expect to incur tariff expense of approximately $2 million to $3 million in each of the third and fourth quarters."
  • To mitigate, Onto is "installing manufacturing capability alongside partners into several Asian markets" to support a regional manufacturing strategy and expects tariff exposure to become negligible in 2026.
  • Customer demand variability and timing of AI packaging and advanced node investments represent ongoing risks to revenue predictability.
  • The Semilab acquisition is pending regulatory approval, with integration and realization of synergies still to be detailed post-closing.

Final Takeaway

Onto Innovation's Q2 2025 call underscored strong progress in new product adoption, a robust pipeline for AI logic packaging, and the expected accretive impact of the Semilab acquisition. Management anticipates a significant revenue rebound in Q4, driven by at least 50% growth in AI logic packaging and a return to earlier revenue levels, while actively addressing tariff-related risks through expanded regional manufacturing. The company remains focused on operational resilience and expanding its global footprint as it heads into 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tripadvisor outlines 5%–7% full-year revenue growth as AI integration and experiences drive group strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Tripadvisor (TRIP) Q2 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Matthew Goldberg stated that "Group revenue grew 7% or 5% in constant currency to $529 million at the upper end of our range. Adjusted EBITDA of $107 million or 20% of revenue exceeded our expectations." Goldberg emphasized the company's "progress to transform Tripadvisor Group, as evidenced in the shift in our portfolio mix," noting that growth marketplaces Viator and TheFork now constitute nearly 60% of revenue, growing at an 18% CAGR, and together added more than $75 million of adjusted EBITDA over the last 12 months compared to an adjusted EBITDA loss of $61 million two years ago.

  • Goldberg identified experiences as "the fastest-growing category in travel" and described a strategic shift: "We see an opportunity to shift away from optimizing for individual brand strategies and operate with an eye towards deeper coordination... to leverage our group-wide assets across our marketing channels, customer traffic, supply relationships, product, data and technology to drive both growth and efficiency." He detailed early performance gains from coordinated marketing optimization across Tripadvisor and Viator.

  • The CEO highlighted the integration of AI, stating "AI is enabling us to build better experiences faster," and described its application across product personalization, search, and operational efficiency. Goldberg also pointed to key product updates: "At Viator, we're rolling out the use of AI to refine the relevance of search results, improving booking initiation and conversion. At TheFork, we're testing conversational AI to help diners quickly find the right restaurant match and expand our partner base."

  • CFO Michael Noonan reported, "Consolidated revenue in Q2 was in line with expectations at $529 million, a growth of 7% or 5% in constant currency. Consolidated adjusted EBITDA of $107 million or 20% of revenue exceeded expectations, primarily due to more favorable marketing efficiencies at Viator."

  • Noonan noted that Viator's adjusted EBITDA reached $32 million or 12% of revenue, representing a margin improvement of nearly 800 basis points, while TheFork's adjusted EBITDA was $9 million or 16% of revenue, with a margin improvement of nearly 900 basis points.

Outlook

  • Noonan stated, "For Q3, we expect consolidated revenue growth of 4% to 6% and an adjusted EBITDA margin of between 19% and 21%." For Viator, "we expect 16% to 18% growth in the number of experiences booked and total segment revenue growth in the high single digits." TheFork is expected to deliver "revenue growth of approximately 25% to 27%, which includes approximately 8 percentage points of currency benefit at current rates."

  • For the full year, guidance remains unchanged: "5% to 7% revenue growth and 16% to 18% adjusted EBITDA margin. The revenue range assumes the impact of traffic headwinds at Brand Tripadvisor remains stable."

Financial Results

  • Group revenue for Q2 was $529 million, with consolidated adjusted EBITDA of $107 million. Viator revenue rose 11% to $270 million, and TheFork revenue grew 28% to $54 million. Brand Tripadvisor revenue was $242 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $66 million, or 27% of revenue.

  • Viator reported 15% growth in experiences booked, with gross booking value up 13% to approximately $1.3 billion. TheFork's B2B revenue more than doubled year-over-year, and nearly 80% of bookings now come through the app and from repeat diners.

  • Operating cash flow for Q2 was $202 million, with free cash flow at $177 million. Cash and cash equivalents totaled approximately $1.2 billion, including $350 million in Term Loan B proceeds.

Q&A

  • Richard J. Clarke, Bernstein: Questioned whether ongoing free traffic headwinds at Brand Tripadvisor would affect stabilization expectations for 2026 and asked about engagement with new shareholders. Michael Noonan: "Some of the free traffic headwinds has persisted. I don't think we are necessarily -- it's too early to call 2026... but look forward to doing the work and thinking about how we improve operational efficiencies as we look to our planning, which we are in the middle of right now." Matthew Goldberg: "We value constructive engagement with all of our shareholders and appreciate their feedback."

  • Nafeesa Gupta, BofA Securities: Asked about the mix between 3P, Viator Origin and Triple Origin for experiences and confidence behind expected revenue reacceleration in Q4. Noonan: "3P is a diverse channel... growth in 3P channel... is being driven by our OTA merchant partners... These are additive to revenue growth... highly accretive to our profitability." On Q4, "We see good bookings that are extending and past Q3 into Q4... so we feel pretty confident about that as we turn to Q4."

  • Vince Charles Ciepiel, Cleveland Research: Inquired about recent app changes and plans to roll out to other geographies. Goldberg: "We're really excited about the work that we're doing in the TripAdvisor app... While we don't break out the actual percentage as a total... we are seeing growth there really strong... We're in the U.S., but we think we'll roll that out further over time."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a neutral to slightly negative tone regarding traffic headwinds, the timing of stabilization for Brand Tripadvisor, and the sustainability of growth rates, pressing management for details and clarification.

  • Management maintained a confident but measured tone, acknowledging persisting challenges and emphasizing operational improvement, group-wide coordination, and continued investment in AI and product enhancements. Goldberg stated, "We're not only successfully integrating AI into our products, we're actively shaping how AI will define the future of travel."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment was slightly more cautious on Brand Tripadvisor’s growth prospects, while management’s tone remained optimistic but realistic, with a greater emphasis on cross-brand coordination and operational efficiencies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth for Q2 was 7% compared to 1% in Q1. Adjusted EBITDA margin rose to 20% from 11% in Q1. Viator shifted from an EBITDA loss in Q1 to a substantial profit in Q2.

  • Strategic focus shifted further toward operational coordination between Viator and Tripadvisor, aiming to maximize combined experience revenue growth and market share.

  • Analysts’ focus sharpened on the persistence of traffic headwinds and revenue mix, while management’s confidence in AI integration and group synergies became more pronounced.

  • TheFork demonstrated accelerating revenue and margin improvement compared to Q1, with B2B revenues and app adoption both rising.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing "free traffic headwinds" at Brand Tripadvisor, noting continued pressure on legacy revenue streams.

  • Noonan indicated that a higher mix of third-party merchant bookings at Viator is impacting average booking value and take rate, though these bookings are "immediately profitable."

  • The company continues to monitor macroeconomic pressures and competitive dynamics, with a focus on maintaining marketing efficiency and operational discipline.

Final Takeaway

Tripadvisor emphasized solid revenue growth and strategic progress in Q2 2025, fueled by strong results at Viator and TheFork and continued investment in AI and operational coordination. The group reaffirmed its full-year guidance of 5% to 7% revenue growth and 16% to 18% adjusted EBITDA margin, citing early performance gains from deeper cross-brand alignment and confidence in its position to capture durable growth in the experiences segment, despite ongoing traffic headwinds at Brand Tripadvisor.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Aemetis targets 550,000 MMBtu RNG production capacity in 2025 as regulatory credits drive growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Aemetis, Inc. (AMTX) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Eric A. McAfee, Chairman and CEO, reported that "in our dairy RNG business, we are steadily scaling up gas production with a new multi-dairy digester coming online this month that is expected to increase RNG production by 30%." He outlined expectations to reach 550,000 MMBtus of renewable natural gas production capacity this year and grow to a 1 million MMBtu annual run rate by the end of 2026. McAfee highlighted the approval of 7 dairy pathways by CARB, noting this unlocks about 120% more LCFS credit revenue for those dairies starting this quarter. He also referenced the expansion into ethanol production in India and the appointment of a new CFO at the India subsidiary as steps toward an IPO targeted for early 2026.

  • McAfee emphasized that amendments to California's LCFS program effective July 1 have raised credit prices from about $42 to $60, with the current cap at $268 for 2025. He stated, "collectively, molecule revenues, LCFS credit sales, D3 RIN sales and the sale of 45Z production tax credits are expected to generate strong positive cash flow from operations this year, and expanding operating cash flow in 2026 as new production comes online."

  • Todd Waltz, CFO, stated "revenues were $52.2 million, up by $9.3 million from the first quarter revenue, primarily due to the fulfillment of biodiesel orders with oil marketing companies in India." Waltz also noted, "operating loss improved by $4.9 million from the first quarter of 2025, reflecting reduced SG&A in the second quarter of 2025."

Outlook

  • Management expects multiple revenue streams from India, LCFS credits, and federal tax incentives to ramp up as the year progresses, positioning Aemetis for a stronger second half of 2025. McAfee stated, "we expect further strengthening during the second half of 2025 and for the foreseeable future."

  • The company is targeting an IPO of its India subsidiary in early 2026 and anticipates utilizing proceeds partly for debt repayment and development of India-based assets. McAfee explained, "I would anticipate probably 25% of the IPO proceeds would be for the parent company and the balance will be for development of the India assets."

  • Section 45Z production tax credits are expected to become a recurring revenue stream, with McAfee projecting, "the current calculation should be about $82 per MMBtu."

Financial Results

  • Aemetis reported revenues of $52.2 million for Q2 2025. California Ethanol production was 13.8 million gallons for the quarter. California Dairy Renewable Natural Gas recognized $3.1 million of revenue from 11 operating digesters during Q2. Interest expense, excluding Series A preferred unit accretion, was $12.3 million. The company reported a net loss of $23.4 million. Cash at year-end was $1.6 million following investment in carbon intensity reduction and dairy renewable natural gas production expansion.

  • Operating loss improved by $4.9 million from the first quarter, attributed to reduced SG&A.

Q&A

  • Matthew Blair, Tudor, Pickering, Holt & Co. Securities: "Congrats on getting the CARB approval for the 7 dairies at the end of the second quarter. I think you mentioned that, that did not flow into your financial results. Could you talk about at current LCFS prices around $60, what kind of EBITDA impact that might have for Aemetis?" McAfee responded that the financial impact is highly correlated with the price of the credits and promised to provide a memo on the range of values per MMBtu for the LCFS as prices change.

  • Blair also asked about D3 RIN supply-demand outlook. McAfee stated the industry believes the D3 RIN is being understated and that public comments are focusing on actual renewable fuel standard regulation requirements.

  • Derrick Whitfield, Texas Capital: "Could you update us on the progress of 45Z as you understand it...?" McAfee explained that an update to the GREET model is expected soon, enabling the generation and sale of 45Z credits in August or September. He indicated the current target value for 45Z is $82 per MMBtu.

  • Amit Dayal, H.C. Wainwright: "Just a question on the monetization strategy for your production tax credits going forward. It's been a little lumpy in the past. But going forward, with this regulatory backdrop, do you think we can see more consistent monetization?" McAfee said, "45Z should become a recurring quarterly revenue item."

  • David Storms, Stonegate: "Could you remind us with your RNGs that are expected to get approval by the end of this year, are those approvals backwards looking?" McAfee stated, "the 4 that are pending would be expected to be approved next year and pack back to best case scenario is probably the fourth quarter of this year."

  • Edward Moon Woo, Ascendiant Capital: "If it gets passed, how quickly do you think it will get implemented? And how quickly will you see the increase in demand for your ethanol?" McAfee replied, "implementation will not be immediate at every one of the stations in California" and described an 18-month cycle before significant need for additional production capacity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on regulatory tailwinds, monetization of credits, and India expansion, with a generally positive to slightly positive tone, expressing interest in the financial impacts of recent credit approvals and production tax credit monetization.

  • Management tone was confident and forward-looking during prepared remarks, particularly on regulatory and market developments. In the Q&A, McAfee maintained a detailed and proactive tone, occasionally deferring to future updates for financial specifics. He used phrases like "we are steadily scaling up", "we expect further strengthening", and "we are very deep in a refinancing process" that signal confidence.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts showed heightened optimism about revenue streams from credits and India. Management's tone shifted towards greater assurance, especially as regulatory approvals materialized.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q2 guidance language was stronger and more specific, with explicit mention of a 30% RNG production increase, 550,000 MMBtu capacity target for 2025, and new revenue streams from Section 45Z credits.

  • Strategic focus expanded to include a more aggressive India IPO timeline and ethanol production plans in India, as well as confirmation of a new CFO for the subsidiary.

  • Key metrics showed revenue growth versus the prior quarter, reflecting resumed biodiesel orders and higher RNG output.

  • Management's confidence increased compared to Q1, with more definitive statements about regulatory impacts and monetization timelines. Analysts' tone was more focused on timing and financial realization of regulatory credits, compared to prior questions about operational progress and risk mitigation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Waltz highlighted that Section 45Z tax credit revenue from dairy RNG production was not included in Q2 results as revenue is recognized when credits are sold.

  • McAfee cautioned that the financial impact of LCFS pathway approvals is highly dependent on fluctuating credit prices and regulatory timing.

  • Ongoing regulatory clarifications, particularly concerning 45Z credits and GREET model updates, present uncertainty for revenue recognition.

  • The India IPO process is in preparatory stages, with market and regulatory conditions influencing use of proceeds and timing.

Final Takeaway

Aemetis reported revenue growth in Q2 2025, driven by resumed biodiesel orders in India and increased RNG output, while advancing key regulatory approvals and scaling up capacity. Management expects significant cash flow improvements as LCFS credit prices rise and federal tax credits are monetized, targeting 550,000 MMBtu of RNG production this year. Strategic initiatives include progress on an India IPO and expansion into ethanol production, supported by favorable legislative developments in both the U.S. and India. Execution on credit monetization, regulatory clarity, and project ramp-up remain central to Aemetis’ trajectory through the rest of 2025 and into 2026.
